summary: Steganographic code execution from logo.png on require
details: On require, vite-plugin-logo walks up to 5 parent directories searching for public/assets/logo.png, scans the PNG bytes for the marker '__VITE_ASSET_CACHE_v1__', base64-decodes the trailing payload, and executes it via `new Function('require', code)(require)` (index.js:28-30). The execution path is wrapped in `try {... } catch (e) {}` so any errors are silently swallowed (index.js:13,33), variable names are single letters (r, l, c, d, m, i) to obscure intent, and the marker string masquerades as a Vite asset-cache feature. The advertised purpose — a Vite plugin to optimize a logo asset — does not require evaluating bytes embedded in a PNG. This is a stego-dropper: arbitrary JavaScript can be hidden in any logo.png placed under the consumer's project tree and will run with full Node privileges and access to `require` whenever the plugin is imported (e.g., during `vite build` or `vite dev`).

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
